channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rare Behind-the-Scenes Portraits Showcase Joan Collins During the Filming of 'Land of the Pharaohs' in 1955
item.description: This article from Vintage Everyday highlights a collection of rare behind-the-scenes photographs of actress Joan Collins taken during the production of the 1955 historical epic 'Land of the Pharaohs',...

item.title: Jim Acosta and Jamie Raskin Criticize Trump's Handling of Federal Property and Reflecting Pool Vandalism Claims
item.description: Former CNN host Jim Acosta, now hosting his own show, discussed President Donald Trump's recent threats to prosecute individuals for damaging the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool during a conversation...

item.title: Criticism Mounts Over Stephen Miller Social Media Post Interpreted as Extremist Rhetoric
item.description: White House Deputy Chief of Staff Stephen Miller has sparked widespread criticism following a brief post on X (formerly Twitter) that read, 'Change the voters, change the country.' The statement quick...

item.title: Hope Sandoval's Reserved and Ethereal Stage Presence with Mazzy Star in the 1990s
item.description: In the 1990s, Hope Sandoval emerged as one of the most distinctive figures in alternative rock as the lead singer of Mazzy Star. Known for her understated performances, Sandoval often stood nearly mot...

item.title: Nancy Allen’s 1977 Promotional Photo Session for the Film 'I Wanna Hold Your Hand'
item.description: In 1977, actress Nancy Allen participated in a promotional photo session with photographer KC Bryant in Burbank, California, tied to her role in the upcoming 1978 film 'I Wanna Hold Your Hand.' At the...

item.title: Australia Expands H5N1 Monitoring as Bird Flu Cases Spread to a Second State
item.description: Australian authorities have intensified surveillance and testing efforts after South Australia became the second state to detect the highly pathogenic H5N1 bird flu virus. The latest case was identifi...

item.title: Federal judge permanently blocks Trump executive order on election procedures
item.description: A federal court has issued a major ruling against a Trump-era executive order related to voting and election procedures, permanently blocking its implementation. U.S. District Court Judge Denise Caspe...

item.title: Trump Delays Housing Bill Signing Ceremony, Prompting Questions From Congressional Republicans
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson defended President Donald Trump after the president unexpectedly canceled a planned signing ceremony for the bipartisan 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act. The event had been ...

item.title: Washington D.C. Residents Express Frustration Over Construction Disruptions for Freedom 250 Celebrations on the National Mall
item.description: Washington, D.C. locals and visitors are voicing their annoyance as extensive construction projects transform the National Mall in preparation for President Donald Trump's Freedom 250 celebrations. Th...

item.title: Trump Cancels Housing Bill Signing Ceremony and Ties Approval to Passage of SAVE America Act
item.description: President Donald Trump unexpectedly canceled a scheduled signing ceremony for a bipartisan housing bill, leaving several lawmakers unaware of the decision until they arrived at Statuary Hall in the U....

item.title: SpaceX Tests Starfall Reentry Vehicle for Rapid Global Cargo Delivery and Space Manufacturing Missions
item.description: SpaceX has conducted the first demonstration flight of Starfall, a newly revealed reentry vehicle designed to transport cargo from low-Earth orbit back to Earth on rapid timelines. The mission launche...

item.title: Startup uses light-activated biodegradable polymer to improve peripheral nerve repair after surgery
item.description: A French biotech startup, Tissium, is developing a light-activated biodegradable polymer designed to improve how surgeons repair damaged peripheral nerves. Each year, around 500,000 people in the Unit...

item.title: Senate hearing questions USPS proposal on mail-in ballots and voter roll requirements
item.description: During a heated Senate Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs Committee hearing, Postmaster General David Steiner faced sharp questioning over a controversial proposed rule involving mail-in ballo...

item.title: Clive Davis, influential music executive and talent discoverer, dies at 94
item.description: Clive Davis, one of the most influential figures in the modern music industry and the executive behind the rise of stars such as Whitney Houston, Bruce Springsteen, Alicia Keys, and Barry Manilow, has...

item.title: DOJ asks Ninth Circuit to vacate Jan. 6 rioter’s firearm conviction tied to domestic violence record
item.description: The Trump Department of Justice has filed a renewed motion asking a federal appeals court to overturn the firearm conviction of Benjamin John Martin, a man convicted of illegally possessing guns follo...

item.title: Global Technology Stock Sell-Off Pushes Nasdaq Down 2% as Semiconductor Shares Decline Sharply
item.description: U.S. stock markets ended lower on Tuesday as a broad global sell-off in technology shares intensified, led by significant declines in semiconductor and memory-chip companies. The Nasdaq Composite drop...

item.title: Overview of Nikolai Sverchkov’s Equestrian and Winter Landscape Paintings
item.description: The article presents an overview of the life and artistic legacy of Nikolai Sverchkov (1817–1898), a prominent 19th-century Russian painter best known for his detailed and atmospheric depictions of ho...

item.title: Lionel Messi inspires nationwide tributes in Argentina during 2026 World Cup after breaking scoring record
item.description: Argentina’s admiration for Lionel Messi has reached extraordinary levels during the 2026 World Cup, with new public tributes highlighting his lasting impact on the country. In the Patagonian town of C...

item.title: A Look at Jeff Beck’s Career and Studio Portraits During His Transformative 1985 Period
item.description: The article presents a collection of 30 studio portraits of legendary English guitarist Jeff Beck taken in 1985 while also examining an important stage in his musical career. Beck, who first gained re...

item.title: Leigh McGowan and Jason Rantz Clash on CNN Over Democratic Politics and Zohran Mamdani
item.description: Political commentator Leigh McGowan and conservative radio host Jason Rantz engaged in a heated debate during a CNN segment moderated by Abby Phillip. The discussion focused on recent Democratic prima...
